title: Time Travel Queries Use Current Schema Instead of Versioned Schema (leading to SEGFAULT)
description:
### What happens?

Hi, 

While testing time travel queries using a table on an Airport server, I noticed that it's currently not possible to return a schema that reflects a previous version of a table. Specifically, when querying a versioned table using `AT (VERSION => X)`, it looks like the current schema from the `TableCatalogEntry` is used, rather than the schema from the specified version.


Here is some example SQL:

```sql
CALL airport_action('grpc://localhost:50003/', 'create_database', 'test1');

ATTACH 'test1' (TYPE  AIRPORT, location 'grpc://localhost:50003/');
CREATE SCHEMA test1.test_schema;

use test1.test_schema;

-- Create a new table on an Airport server that offers time travel and version
-- each commit to the table makes a new version starting from zero.
create table version_test (name text);

insert into version_test (name) values ('Elliott');
insert into version_test (name) values ('Scarlett');

-- Add a new column but check that we can still time travel around.
alter table version_test add column age int;
alter table version_test add column city varchar;
alter table version_test add column zip varchar;

select * from version_test AT (VERSION => 2);
```


At version 2, the table only had the `name` column. However, the query appears to use the current schema (which includes `age`, `city`, and `zip`) - because thats what's in the current `TableCatalogEntry`.  

Problems ensue because the `LogicalGet` is constructed using the current schema of the `TableCatalogEntry` rather than the historical one.  Here is the relevant code:

https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/blob/4e5ff4e5e8508791f2fedbd3c9d19e9e5a5e4d93/src/planner/binder/tableref/bind_basetableref.cpp#L300-L314

Would it make sense to extend the `GetScanFunction` to allow returning the schema of the table as it existed at the requested version?  

### To Reproduce

See code above.

### OS:

Mac OS X

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.3.0-dev3607 (Development Version) 4e5ff4e5e8

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Rusty Conover

### Affiliation:

None

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

No - Other reason (please specify in the issue body)

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE version_test (name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT, city VARCHAR(100), zip VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO version_test (name, age, city, zip) VALUES ('Elliott', 30, 'New York', '10001');
INSERT INTO version_test (name, age, city, zip) VALUES ('Scarlett', NULL, 'Los Angeles', '90001');
ALTER TABLE version_test ADD COLUMN created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
key_bug_features: Time travel queries use current schema instead of versioned schema
